concerns.text: Task T035 implements a 'Paired T-Test' comparing Heuristic vs. Full-Batch variance. This silently relaxes FR-006 and US-3 which explicitly mandate a 'one-sample t-test' on the deviation from the theoretical bound. The plan.md attempted to justify this as a 'methodological revision' to avoid tautology, but the tasks document must not unilaterally discard a functional requirement (FR-006) without an explicit spec amendment or a task that documents the deviation as a required change to the spec itself.

Task T039 implements 'correlation' analysis between variance error and Pareto distance. This violates SC-002 which mandates that the failure point must 'coincide (within ±1 objective count)' with the Pareto threshold. Replacing a strict coincidence check with a correlation metric is a semantic weakening of the success criterion, effectively lowering the bar for validation without a spec update.

Task T006 (and T026) implements MDP generation but lacks an explicit step to support the noise correlation parameter $ho$ required by FR-009. FR-009 mandates sensitivity analysis on noise correlation ($ho \in \{0, 0.2, 0.5\}$). Deferring this to Phase 4 (T031) creates a foundational gap where the core MDP generator cannot satisfy FR-009 from the start, risking a rework of the foundational phase or a silent failure to meet the requirement.

Task T022 creates a derivation document but omits the 'peer review checklist' artifact required by SC-001 as an alternative verification path. SC-001 states verification can be by 'symbolic math engine OR peer review checklist'. The task list only covers the engine path, silently dropping the alternative verification method mandated by the spec.
